Thought 6: Based on the evidence, Julia has a stronger and more direct motive (financial inheritance and personal grievance), direct access to the murder weapon (snake venom through her profession), and was confirmed to be with the victim at the crime scene (mosque) on the day of the murder. Ronald has access to snake venom but no clear motive related to the murder—his reaction was about unrelated misdemeanors. The narrative emphasizes Julia's motive and opportunity more strongly. Therefore, Julia is the most likely murderer.
